rulings.Case Title: SHU SUN CHEUNG ET AL VS. DAVID E. LEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday June 16, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID LEUNG, FENG CHEN Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amended Complaint GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the first cause of action. The first cause of action, as alleged arises out of protected activity, which is also subject to CC 47 privilege: (a) false claims of nuisance contained in the Notice and malicious prosecution against Plaintiffs, unlawful detainer and civil harassment. FAC 7:7-8; 4:21-22; 4:24-5:19; 5:20-22. Therefore Defendants shifted their burden on the second prong and Plaintiffs failed to sustain their burden under second prong. Motion is DENIED as to the second cause of action. This is a mixed cause of action and Ps have shown that their non-protected activity part of the claim has "minimal merit." See Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169. Plaintiff provided sufficient evidence re: violations of SFRO 37.10(a)(1), (4), (10) and (11). Request for fees is DENIED. = (501/REQ)

PLAINTIFF U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Denise Beatty To 1)Serve A Formal Response To Pltf Demand For Production Of Docs2) Appear At A Specific Date And Time To Produce All Responsive Docs To Pltf Demand For Prodcution Of Docs, Set One And 3) Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3795.00; Mpa GRANTED. Defendant shall appear and produce responsive documents, without objections, on any day set by Plaintiff on a 5-day notice no later than June 29, 2016; or any other date mutually agreed by the parties. Sanctions granted in the amount of $3,795. = (501/REQ)
